course_description.overview: One of the UK's only multi-stage sportive events consisting of 3 x 125km loops through Glastonbury Tor, Somerset Levels, and Sherborne Abbey with 4,570m total elevation.

key_segments.name: Glastonbury Tor
key_segments.description: Iconic climb through historic Somerset landmark.

key_segments.name: Somerset Levels
key_segments.description: Flat to rolling terrain across wetlands and farmland.

key_segments.name: Sherborne Abbey
key_segments.description: Historic area with abbey views and surrounding roads.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Multi-day challenge with significant elevation across three demanding stages on undulating English countryside roads.

logistics.getting_there: Fly into Bristol or Bournemouth airports, then train or drive to Yeovil; free parking at event HQ.
logistics.accommodation: Hotels and B&Bs in Yeovil, Somerset; book early for multi-day event.
logistics.registration_details: Online entry via event website; solo, team, relay options; expect entry fees around £100-200 for multi-stage.
logistics.bike_logistics: Road bike recommended; bike support and mechanical aid on course; transport bike to Yeovil.
logistics.local_considerations: English weather variable in May; rural roads, watch for traffic; under 18s need adult accompaniment.

final_verdict.one_liner: Rare UK multi-stage road sportive through stunning Somerset landscapes.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Endurance road cyclists seeking multi-day challenge with solo/team options.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes for UK riders wanting stage racing format without international travel.
pro_tips: Train for repeated 125km days with climbs.
